settings.title: Marketing Site for Fern and Oak Interiors
settings.summary: We gave a design studio a fast portfolio site they can update themselves without calling us.
settings.description:
Fern and Oak had a beautiful body of work trapped inside a slow template they were afraid to touch. Every photo they wanted to add meant emailing a freelancer and waiting a week, so most finished rooms never made it onto the site at all.

The brief

The studio came to us with a simple ask: a marketing site that showed their interiors at full strength and that the team could update without help. They did not want a content system with a hundred buttons. They wanted to post a room and move on.

We started by cataloguing what they actually published in a year and stripped the page types down to the few that mattered. A project gallery, individual room pages, an about section, and a contact form covered everything they needed.

What we built

We built a lightweight site around a project gallery, with an editor the team uses to post new rooms in minutes. Image handling was the heart of it: we set up a pipeline that resizes and compresses every upload so the photos stay sharp on a large screen but still load quickly on a phone.

  • A filterable project gallery with individual room pages
  • An in-house editor for posting new projects without code
  • Automatic image resizing and compression on upload
  • A contact form wired to the studio inbox

We also wrote a short guide and sat with two staff members until they could publish a project on their own. That handoff mattered more than any feature, because the whole point was to get us out of the loop.

The result

The studio publishes new projects on its own now, usually the same week a job wraps. Inquiries through the contact form roughly doubled in the first two months, which the owner credited to having current work on display rather than a gallery frozen a year in the past.
